title: Why reduce dimensionality? Compression vs. visualization
key_concepts: High-dimensional data suffers from the curse of dimensionality, leading to slower models and higher overfitting risk.
Dimensionality reduction creates a smaller, dense latent representation from the original feature matrix.

The two distinct goals of reduction: Compression (preprocessing to remove noise/redundancy while keeping maximum variance/signal for downstream models) vs. Visualization (mapping data to 2D or 3D spaces to let humans inspect clusters, manifolds, or patterns).

definitions.meaning: A dense, low-dimensional space that captures the underlying structure of the data.

examples: Describe plotting 100-dimensional sensor data into 2D to find failure clusters (visualization) vs compressing it to 10 dimensions before feeding it to a classifier (compression).
relations: Builds on Chapter 7 (feature selection). While selection removes columns, reduction creates entirely new, compressed columns.
exclude: Do not explain specific algorithms here; keep it focused on the *why*.
